headline: HSBC Downgrades Nike to Hold as Turnaround Becomes a Show-Me Story With No Near-Term Catalysts
summary: Nike (NYSE:NKE) stock is under pressure Monday morning after HSBC analyst Erwan Rambourg downgraded Nike to Hold from Buy, slashing his price target from $90 to $48. The stock is trading around $42, already below the new target, and is now down 33% year to date. The downgrade lands at a difficult moment. Nike has ... HSBC Downgrades Nike to Hold as Turnaround Becomes a Show-Me Story With No Near-Term Catalysts

headline: Nike’s innovation chief departs as leadership turnover continues

headline: 3 Cash-Producing Stocks We Keep Off Our Radar
summary: While strong cash flow is a key indicator of stability, it doesn’t always translate to superior returns. Some cash-heavy businesses struggle with inefficient spending, slowing demand, or weak competitive positioning.
